pattern: Retrograde BFS (backward induction) on a game state (mouse_pos, cat_pos, whose_turn); propagate win/loss from terminal states backwards, treating unresolved states with no winning move as draws.
hints: Model the game as a directed graph of states (mouse, cat, turn); the mouse wins immediately when mouse==0, the cat wins when cat==mouse and mouse!=0; states where the same configuration repeats are draws.

Use retrograde (backward) BFS: initialize all terminal-win/loss states, then propagate: a state is a loss for the mover if every successor is a win for the opponent; it is a win if any successor is a loss for the opponent.

Track the degree (remaining unexplored moves) for each state; when a move leading to a Loss is found, mark the parent a Win; when the degree of a Draw state hits 0, it becomes a Loss; surviving states with degree > 0 are Draws (0).

edge_inputs.description: example 1: complex graph, result is draw (0)
cat adjacent to hole but cannot enter — mouse can reach hole, mouse wins
cat can immediately corner the mouse before it reaches hole — cat wins
linear graph 0-1-2-3-4; mouse at 1, cat at 2; mouse can move to hole 0
minimal 3-node graph: hole=0, mouse=1 adjacent to hole, cat=2 not adjacent to 0
graph where cat and mouse start with cat having no path to block hole

stress.description: random connected undirected graph with 10 nodes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 20))
    # Build a random connected undirected graph; cat cannot go to node 0
    adj = [set() for _ in range(n)]
    # Ensure connectivity via a random spanning tree
    nodes = list(range(1, n))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    connected = [0]
    for node in nodes:
        parent = rng.choice(connected)
        adj[parent].add(node)
        adj[node].add(parent)
        connected.append(node)
    # Add some random extra edges
    for _ in range(n):
        u = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        v = rng.randint(0, n-1)
        if u != v:
            adj[u].add(v)
            adj[v].add(u)
    graph = [sorted(adj[i]) for i in range(n)]
    return [graph]
